conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease stage 4 (severe decrease in GFR)
contraindications.clinicalRationale: TZD should be discontinued when eGFR falls below 45 ml/min per 1.73 m2 due to reduced kidney function and potential for fluid retention and cardiovascular complications in advanced CKD
contraindications.riskDescription: Increased risk of fluid retention, heart failure, and cardiovascular complications in patients with severely reduced kidney function
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: DPP-4 inhibitors, insulin, and other glucose-lowering drugs suitable for patients with eGFR <45
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 23: Sulfonylurea — Discontinue when eGFR < 30; Dialysis: Discontinue. TZD (thiazolidinedione) — Discontinue when eGFR < 45; Dialysis: Discontinue.

contraindications.clinicalRationale: TZD is contraindicated in dialysis patients due to the risk of fluid retention and cardiovascular complications in the setting of end-stage renal disease
contraindications.riskDescription: Significant risk of fluid overload, heart failure exacerbation, and cardiovascular events in dialysis-dependent patients
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: DPP-4 inhibitors and insulin are more suitable options for dialysis patients
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 23: TZD (thiazolidinedione) — Discontinue when eGFR < 45; Dialysis: Discontinue.

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Thiazolidinediones are less suitable for patients with heart failure due to their propensity to cause fluid retention and worsen heart failure symptoms
contraindications.riskDescription: Risk of fluid retention leading to heart failure exacerbation or worsening of existing heart failure
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: GLP-1 receptor agonists are more suitable for patients with heart failure
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 25 | For patients with heart failure: GLP-1 RA is more suitable; thiazolidinedione is less suitable.
